title: Croissant ML metadata descriptor for releases
context: ML dataset discovery platforms (Kaggle, Google Dataset Search, Hugging Face) increasingly require or benefit from schema.org/Croissant ML metadata descriptors. Adding a Croissant manifest to each corpus release improves discoverability for ML/NLP consumers, which is a core beneficiary group. The descriptor must accurately reflect the license tiers in the corpus — in particular, it must not represent the whole corpus as a single license when it is a multi-tier mixed-license collection.
objective: Implement a Croissant ML metadata descriptor generator that emits a schema.org/Croissant-compatible manifest for each corpus release, accurately representing the multi-tier license structure and corpus composition.
acceptanceCriteria: Generates a valid schema.org/Croissant manifest for each corpus release, covering dataset name, description, DOI/URL, license tiers (accurately representing the multi-tier structure — not a single merged license), provenance, and record count per tier.
The Croissant manifest is committed alongside each release and validates against the official Croissant schema.
The manifest accurately represents each licenseTier as a separate RecordSet or distribution, so ML consumers can select only the tiers they are licensed to use.
A note is added to the datasheet explaining how to interpret the Croissant manifest for multi-license corpora.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off; code MIT licensed.
output: A merged PR adding a Croissant manifest generator (TypeScript/ESM) that emits a schema.org/Croissant-compatible manifest for each corpus release, with CI validation and documentation of the multi-tier license representation.
